name: Bhai Harbans Singh Suraj (UK)
body: Bhai Harbans Singh Ji was born on the 12th of August 1950 in Alwar, Rajasthan. His father, Sant Baba Ajit Singh Ji was originally from the Sindh region which is now in Pakistan. Baba Ajit Singh Ji served as an officer in the police force but he was also an expert harmonium and tabla player. He has played tabla with many prominent singers and saints in Sindh, such as Sant Kanwar Ram Ji, Satou Bhagat, Motan Bhagat, Prahlad Bhagat, Sant Dandu Ram and Tilly Bai Ji. He has also accompanied Bibi Mansha Kaur Ji who was married to the renowned taar shehnai maestro, Bhai Piara Singh Ji. Having been born into such an atmosphere, Bhai Harbans Singh Ji naturally began learning music from a very young age. He was initiated into music by his father and then went on to learn the intricacies of classical music from the late Maharaj Mool Chand Ji from Bhilwara. He also learned from Master Navrang Chand Ji and Mr. Shobha Ram Ji from Bhilwara. Aside from these maestros, he was also heavily influenced by Bhai Gurmukh Singh Suraj from Kota, who was a close friend and mentor to him. In honour of his late mentor, Bhai Harbans Singh Ji adapted the title ‘Suraj’.

Bhai Harbans Singh Ji’s first musical performance was in 1956 at the age of 6. Along with his music education, he also went on to complete his formal education. After high school, he graduated with a Bachelor of Arts from the University of Rajasthan. He served with the late Giani Sant Singh Ji Maskeen for many years and accompanied him on many tours. Following Maskeen Ji’s advice, he served on the side harmonium with Bhai Kalyan Singh Ji (Lucknow wale) for many years. During their time together, they toured Afghanistan, the US and England. In 1981, Bhai Harbans Singh Ji migrated to the UK and became a British citizen. He has been serving the ‘Sangat’ of UK and other parts of Europe since then.

He is fluent in many languages including Punjabi, Hindi, Guaarki, Sindhi, Urdu and English. He uses this skill to his advantage to join members of the Sangat from various communities with the ‘Shabad’ of our Gurus. Being from the Guaar community (root from Suryavanshi Rajput), he has heavily contributed to the development of the Guaarki language and history. Bhai Harbans Singh Ji developed the writing system for the Guaarki language consisting of 52 alphabets. He has also written several books on the Guaar community consisting of their history, language and poetry. Apart from that, he was trained in classical music and he is adept at performing in various rare Raags and Taals. He has created over 6000 compositions and has performed on various television and radio channels including TV Asia, Zee TV, Vectone TV, DM Digital TV and BBC Radio. He has recorded ‘Mool Mantar’ in 56 languages and was appreciated by Queen Elizabeth and Prince Charles for this effort. One of his unique qualities is his interest in collecting Kirtan recordings, antiques and world bank notes. He has a vast collection of recordings and is one of my main inspirations to start the ‘Kirtan Sewa’ channel on Youtube and this website as well.

Bhai Harbans Singh Ji is the oldest of seven siblings, and his youngest brother, Bhai Jogender Singh Jogi accompanies him regularly on tabla. Bhai Jogender Singh Ji was born on 1st January 1968 and their duo is popularly known as the jatha of ‘Suraj-Jogi’. Bhai Harbans Singh Suraj got married to Sardarni Bhagwant Kaur Ji on 26th November 1972 and they are blessed with three daughters and one son. If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Harbans Singh Ji Suraj, please contact us.
